DEFENDANT KOHL'S SHALL PROVIDE VERIFIED, OBJECTION-FREE RESPONSES TO ALL DISCOVERY REQUESTS WITHIN 14 DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE ORDER SHALL BE LODGED BY EMAIL IN WORD (.DOC) FORMAT DIRECTLY TO JUDGE PRO TEM GEERHART. JUDGE PRO TEM: CHARLES GEERHART; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set Two And Motion For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Oct-19-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Kevin J. McInerney of the law offices of Kevin J. McInerney (775-849-3811) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/moving party

Attorney, Tracie Lynn Childs of the law offices of Ogletree Deakins Nash Smoak & Stewart PC (619-595-4837) appears as counsel for defendant/opposing party

Counsel for defendant declines stipulation to judge pro tem. Court and cousel confer regarding tentative rulings. Court finds there was no notice of contesting the tentative ruling and deems the parties to have stipulated per CRC 3.1308 and Local Rule 8.3 Tentative Rulings.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFF CLARISSA DUKE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Special Interrogatories, Set Two And Motion For Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED. Defendant Kohl's shall provide verified, objection-free responses within 14 days of service of this order. Motion granted. Defendant Kohl's shall provide verified, objection-free responses to all discovery requests within 14 days of service of this order. Request for Sanctions is DENIED. The Court finds there was inadequate effort made by moving party to engage in the meet and confer process before filing the motion, and there is evidence Kohl's made a reasonable effort to resolve this dispute informally. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312. The order shall be lodged by email in Word (.doc) format directly to Judge Pro Tem Geerhart.

Judge Pro Tem: Charles Geerhart; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302)
